text.title: Gcp Resource Hierarchy Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-resource-hierarchy methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Projects bypass folder-inherited policies; drift hotspots.
antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud projects list` shows projects directly under organisation node.
antipattern.fix: Move projects under appropriate folder; rerun org policy verification.

antipattern.symptom: Cost roll-ups fragment; FinOps allocation hard.
antipattern.root-cause: Each project has its own billing account assignment.
antipattern.fix: Assign billing accounts at folder level; remove per-project overrides.

antipattern.symptom: Inventory + scripting hard; project-id lookups fail.
antipattern.root-cause: Project IDs across BU/env don't follow a pattern.
antipattern.fix: Adopt `{env}-{bu}-{service}-{purpose}` convention; rename non-compliant projects on next cycle.

antipattern.symptom: Single-level folders mean policies are too coarse OR too fine.
antipattern.root-cause: All projects in one root-level folder.
